mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during tightening periods yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that compress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a falling unemployment rate creates stagflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and falling UNRATE predicts positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth during a post-shock recovery phase signals accelerating economic normalization that boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth > 200k during recovery phases fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the index of industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing activity that immediately reduces near-term earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
